>
> Do NOT use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` — `IsTestProject` is not defined when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.

### 3. Add MTP extension NuGet packages to each test project
Because you use TRX reporting and code coverage, add:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

If using Central Package Management, add version entries to `Directory.Packages.props` instead.

Optional (only if your CI uses blame/crash/hang):

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.2" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump" Version="1.6.2" />
```

### 4. Verify local build & test
```bash
dotnet build MySolution.sln
dotnet test MySolution.sln --no-build -- --report-trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

Compare pass/fail/skip counts against the Phase 1 baseline.

Also run a test executable directly to confirm MTP works standalone:
```bash
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe
```

### 5. Update Azure DevOps pipeline

#### 5a. Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --no-build --configuration Release
      --
      --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

#### 5b. CLI argument translation

| VSTest / old dotnet test         | MTP equivalent (after `--`)                       |
|----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `--logger trx`                   | `--report-trx`                                     |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"`      | `--coverage`                                       |
| `--collect "...;Format=cobertura"` | `--coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura`  |
| `--results-directory <DIR>`      | `--results-directory <DIR>`                        |
| `--filter <EXPR>`               | `--filter <EXPR>` (identical for MSTest)           |
| `--settings <FILE>`             | `--settings <FILE>`                                |
| `--blame-crash`                  | `--crashdump` (requires CrashDump package)         |
| `--blame-hang`                   | `--hangdump` (requires HangDump package)           |
| `--blame-hang-timeout 5m`       | `--hangdump-timeout 5m`                            |

> **Important**: On .NET 8/9 SDK, all MTP-specific args must come **after** `--`. Build-level args (`-c`, `-f`, `--no-build`, `-v`) go before `--`.

#### 5c. Publish test results & coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '**/*.trx'
    searchFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)'
    mergeTestResults: true
  condition: always()

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

### 6. Handle exit code 8 (zero tests discovered)
MTP returns exit code 8 when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). If you have conditional/filtered runs that may match nothing, add to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

Only add this if you have legitimate zero-test scenarios. Otherwise, exit code 8 is a useful early warning.

### 7. Clean up VSTest-only packages (after verification)
Once everything works end-to-end in CI, remove from each test project:
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed when `EnableMSTestRunner` is active

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` (or the `MSTest` meta-package).

> Only remove after full CI verification. If you need to roll back, re-add these packages and revert `Directory.Build.props`.

### 8. Validate end-to-end
- [ ] `dotnet build` — zero errors across all 5 projects
- [ ] `dotnet test` — all tests pass; counts match Phase 1 baseline
- [ ] Test executable runs directly (e.g., `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe`)
- [ ] CI pipeline produces TRX and code coverage artifacts
- [ ] Visual Studio / VS Code Test Explorer discovers and runs tests
- [ ] No `VSTest@3` or `vstest.console.exe` references remain in CI

---

## Key Gotchas
| Pitfall | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Mixing VSTest + MTP projects | Migrate all 5 together — mixed mode is unsupported |
| `dotnet test` MTP args ignored | Must use `--` separator on .NET 8/9 SDK |
| Exit code 8 in CI | MTP fails on zero tests; use `--ignore-exit-code 8` if needed |
| `IsTestProject` condition in Directory.Build.props | Not defined at import time; use `MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith(...)` |
| Missing `OutputType Exe` | Required for MTP; set in Directory.Build.props |
| `--filter` syntax | Unchanged for MSTest — no rewriting needed |

## Future (.NET 10 SDK)
When you upgrade to .NET 10+, simplify:
1. Remove `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` from `Directory.Build.props`
2. Drop the `--` separator in CLI commands
3. Add to `global.json`: `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }`

## Rollback
Revert `Directory.Build.props` changes, restore `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` + `MSTest.TestAdapter` packages, switch pipeline back to `VSTest@3`."}
